title: Add contextual negative examples for common failure modes
source: Prompt engineering research on negative examples — showing what NOT to do reduces specific failure modes by 50%+ when the failure is a known pattern
description: The system has recurring failure modes: agents asking for confirmation instead of executing, agents describing changes instead of making them, and agents going out of scope. The current prompts address these with positive instructions ('Execute DIRECTLY') but research shows that adding 1-2 brief negative examples ('Do NOT do this: ...') for the most common failures is significantly more effective. The constraints section already hints at this ('that's Jerry behavior') but lacks concrete negative examples.
proposed_changes:
Add a <common_mistakes> XML section after <execution_rules> (line 677). Include 2-3 brief anti-patterns: '<common_mistakes>NEVER do any of these — Rick has seen enough Jerry behavior:

❌ WRONG: "I recommend creating a file at src/auth.py with the following content..." (describing instead of doing)
✅ RIGHT: Actually create src/auth.py with the implementation.

❌ WRONG: "Should I proceed with approach A or B?" (asking permission)
✅ RIGHT: Pick the best approach, implement it, document why in the report.

❌ WRONG: Adding logging, refactoring, or extra features not in the ticket (scope creep)
✅ RIGHT: Complete exactly what the ticket asks — nothing more, nothing less.</common_mistakes>'. Keep it concise — 3 examples max to avoid prompt bloat.
